projectDescription: MCP Server for Computer Use in Windows - enables AI agents to interact with the Windows operating system through 15 automation tools.
keyFeatures: Seamless Windows Integration - Interacts natively with Windows UI elements, opens apps, controls windows, simulates user input
Works with Any LLM - No computer vision or fine-tuned models required, reducing complexity and setup time
Rich Toolset - 15 tools for UI automation including keyboard, mouse, window management, and system operations
Lightweight & Open-Source - Minimal dependencies with full source code under MIT license
Real-Time Interaction - 0.2-0.9 second latency between actions depending on system load
DOM Mode for Browsers - Special mode for web automation that filters browser UI for cleaner web page interaction
Local & Remote Modes - Run directly on Windows or connect to cloud-hosted Windows VMs via windowsmcp.io
Multiple Transport Options - Supports stdio, SSE, and streamable HTTP for flexible integration
publicApiSurface: Snapshot - Capture desktop state, UI elements, and screenshots
Click - Perform mouse clicks at coordinates or UI elements
Type - Enter text at specified locations with keyboard simulation
Scroll - Scroll vertically or horizontally in windows and regions
Move - Move mouse cursor or perform drag-and-drop operations
Shortcut - Execute keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+C, Alt+Tab, etc.)
Wait - Pause execution for specified duration
App - Launch applications, resize windows, and switch focus
Shell - Execute PowerShell commands with full system access
FileSystem - Read, write, copy, move, delete, list, and search files
Scrape - Fetch and extract web page content from URLs or browser DOM
MultiSelect - Select multiple items like files or checkboxes
MultiEdit - Enter text into multiple input fields
Clipboard - Read and set Windows clipboard content
Process - List running processes and terminate by PID or name
Registry - Read, write, delete, and list Windows Registry keys and values
